title: argemone mexicana var. subinermis
intro: Argemone mexicana var. subinermis, also known as Mexican prickly poppy or Mexican poppy, is a plant species of the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Argemone mexicana var. subinermis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and provide relief from digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
